item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

item.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount’s $110B Warner Bros. deal
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The states allege that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

item.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

item.title: As TV-tracking app TV Time shuts down, its founder builds Bingers, a new home for fans
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The creator of TV Time is building a successor app that will let users import their watch histories and preserve the community that formed around discussing their favorite shows.

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: SpaceX cleared to fly Starship again after booster failure in May
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: This will be the first Starship test flight for SpaceX as a public company, testing the market's appetite for the company's "fly, fail, fix" approach to rocket development, which often ends in fireballs.

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

item.title: LAPD lets contract with surveillance giant Flock expire, citing ‘serious concerns’ over civil liberties and privacy
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The LAPD, one of Flock's biggest government customers, is ending its contract with the company citing civil liberties concerns.

item.title: Uber’s robotaxi lobbying effort puts it on a collision course with Waymo
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Washington, D.C. has become a battleground for Uber and Waymo's competing views.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A robotaxi ultimatum
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Reed Jobs would rather talk about curing cancer than his last name
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: When we last sat down with Jobs at TechCrunch Disrupt nearly three years ago, his firm Yosemite was brand new and biotech was still reeling from its post-pandemic crash. Now, the venture outfit has a team of 17; a cluster of blockbuster drugs are all losing patent protection in roughly the same window, creating all kinds of new opportunities; and AI has gone from a curiosity to, in Jobs' words, a huge part of what Yosemite does. "I didn't expect Yosemite to be moving this fast," he said.

item.title: This slushie machine was a lifesaver during NYC’s heat wave
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Ninja's latest slushie machine builds on the popularity of the original Slushi, but with a big upgrade.

item.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

item.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

item.title: US cybersecurity agency CISA had to build its incident playbook during the incident, agency reveals
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Independent cybersecurity journalist Brian Krebs reported in May that a security researcher with cyber firm GitGuardian alerted him to reams of exposed passwords stored in a publicly accessible GitHub repository, which an employee of a CISA contractor had uploaded.

item.title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Phia, the shopping startup founded by Bill Gates’ daughter, Phoebe, and her friend Sophia Kianni is under fire for a practice known as “cookie stuffing,” which helped the product receive commissions and credit for sales it did not actually generate, per a Bloomberg investigation.

item.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

item.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.
